segments.text: So they brought the ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tabernacle that David had erected for it. Then they offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.
And when David had finished offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings,
he blessed the people in the name of the Lord.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then he distributed to everyone of Israel, both man and woman, to everyone a loaf of bread, a piece of meat, and a cake of raisins.
And he appointed some of the Levites to minister before the ark of the Lord, to commemorate, to thank, and to praise the Lord God of Israel:
Asaph the chief, and next to him Zechariah, then Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, and Obed-Edom: Jeiel with stringed instruments and harps, but Asaph made music with cymbals;
Benaiah and Jahaziel the priests regularly blew the trumpets before the ark of the covenant of God.
On that day David first delivered this psalm into the hand of Asaph and his brethren, to thank the Lord:
Oh, give thanks to the Lord! Call upon His name; Make known His deeds among the peoples!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Sing to Him, sing psalms to Him; Talk of all His wondrous works!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Glory in His holy name; Let the hearts of those rejoice who seek the Lord!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Seek the Lord and His strength; Seek His face evermore!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Remember His marvelous works which He has done, His wonders, and the judgments of His mouth,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O seed of Israel His servant, You children of Jacob, His chosen ones!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He is the Lord our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Remember His covenant forever, The word which He commanded, for a thousand generations,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The covenant which He made with Abraham, And His oath to Isaac,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And confirmed it to Jacob for a statute, To Israel for an everlasting covenant,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Saying, 'To you I will give the land of Canaan As the allotment of your inheritance,'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When you were few in number, Indeed very few, and strangers in it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When they went from one nation to another, And from one kingdom to another people,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He permitted no man to do them wrong; Yes, He rebuked kings for their sakes,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Saying, 'Do not touch My anointed ones, And do My prophets no harm.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Sing to the Lord, all the earth; Proclaim the good news of His salvation from day to day.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Declare His glory among the nations, His wonders among all peoples.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For the Lord is great and greatly to be praised; He is also to be feared above all gods.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, But the Lord made the heavens.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Honor and majesty are before Him; Strength and gladness are in His place.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Give to the Lord, O families of the peoples, Give to the Lord glory and strength.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Give to the Lord the glory due His name; Bring an offering, and come before Him. Oh, worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Tremble before Him, all the earth. The world also is firmly established, It shall not be moved.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; And let them say among the nations, 'The Lord reigns.'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the sea roar, and all its fullness; Let the field rejoice, and all that is in it.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then the trees of the woods shall rejoice before the Lord, For He is coming to judge the earth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Oh, give thanks to the Lord, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Save us, O God of our salvation; Gather us together, and deliver us from the Gentiles, To give thanks to Your holy name, To triumph in Your praise.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel From everlasting to everlasting!
Amen!
segments.speaker: The people

segments.text: So he left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to minister before the ark regularly, as every day's work required;
and Obed-Edom with his sixty-eight brethren, including Obed-Edom the son of Jeduthun, and Hosah, to be gatekeepers;
and Zadok the priest and his brethren the priests, before the tabernacle of the Lord at the high place that was at Gibeon,
to offer burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar of burnt offering regularly morning and evening, and to do according to all that is written in the Law of the Lord which He commanded Israel;
and with them Heman and Jeduthun and the rest who were chosen, who were designated by name, to give thanks to the Lord, because His mercy endures forever;
and with them Heman and Jeduthun, to sound aloud with trumpets and cymbals and the musical instruments of God. Now the sons of Jeduthun were gatekeepers.
Then all the people departed, every man to his house; and David returned to bless his house.
